title: AdaptiveKV: Accelerating KV Cache Offloading with a Bandwidth-Adaptive Memory Allocation Mechanism
summary: AdaptiveKV accelerates LLM inference by using a bandwidth-adaptive memory allocation mechanism for KV cache offloading across CXL-enabled memory pools. The method achieves a maximum speedup of 1.90× in LLM inference throughput compared to state-of-the-art strategies.

title: BlockServe: Block-Grained Continuous Batching for High-Throughput Diffusion LLM Serving
summary: BlockServe is a serving framework for diffusion LLMs that uses block-grained continuous batching and mixed-state execution to handle convergence heterogeneity, achieving up to 10.6x throughput improvement over prior baselines.

title: StreamDQ: Near-Memory Weight DeQuantization in Custom HBM for Scalable AI Inference Acceleration
summary: StreamDQ introduces near-memory weight dequantization in custom HBM to eliminate GPU-side overhead, achieving up to 7.08x speedup for mixed-precision GEMM and 2.20x higher decode throughput in end-to-end LLM inference.

title: General Non-Clairvoyant KV-Cache Scheduling via Regime-Aware Routing
summary: The paper introduces a non-clairvoyant scheduling algorithm for batched LLM inference that manages requests under a hard KV-cache memory budget to minimize total completion time. It provides the first constant-competitive algorithm for arbitrary prompt and response lengths using a novel regime-aware routing framework of specialized sub-schedulers.

title: Automated Tensor Scheduling for Hybrid CPU-GPU LLM Inference on Consumer Devices
summary: ATSInfer is a hybrid CPU-GPU inference system that performs tensor-granularity offloading combined with load-aware dynamic transfer and asynchronous coordination. The abstract reports up to 1.94x prefill throughput and 3.29x decode throughput improvements over existing systems on consumer devices.

title: Unlocking Parallelism in Autoregressive Language Models via Speculative Decoding with Progressive Tree Drafting
summary: Progressive Tree Drafting (PTD) uses a structured, guided parallel drafting strategy with a progressive tree structure and stepwise pruning to explore multiple paths in a single forward pass. The abstract claims PTD achieves up to 2x decoding speedup while remaining training-free and model-agnostic.

title: TIGER: Text-Conditioned Visual Gated Routing with Acceptance Alignment for Multimodal Speculative Decoding
summary: TIGER introduces a text-conditioned visual gated routing framework for multimodal speculative decoding that dynamically selects sparse visual tokens and applies acceptance-aligned policy training to the drafter. The method is evaluated under exact verifier-side speculative decoding, demonstrating gains in accepted prefix length and speculative speedup while maintaining downstream accuracy.

title: FastTPS: An Optimized Method for LLM Token Phase for AI accelerators
summary: FastTPS accelerates the token-phase in LLM inference via reloading-free KV Cache concatenation, optimized RoPE attention, and a fused MLP pipeline. The method achieves a 6x speedup and 93% peak memory bandwidth utilization on an AMD NPU during Phi3-mini inference.

title: HCRMap: Pressure-Aware Hot-Expert Residency Mapping for 3.5D MoE Chiplet Inference
summary: HCRMap proposes a pressure-aware hot-expert residency mapping framework for 3.5D MoE chiplet inference that dynamically manages expert replicas across memory tiers to mitigate communication and memory bottlenecks. Experiments show the method reduces end-to-end latency by up to 46.7% over baseline methods in both prefill and decode stages.

title: Less Experts, Faster Decoding: Cost-Aware Speculative Decoding for Mixture-of-Experts
summary: EcoSpec is a cost-aware speculative decoding framework for Mixture-of-Experts models that incorporates predicted marginal expert activation costs into draft tree selection to reduce expert scattering. Evaluated on large-scale MoE models, the method reduces active expert footprints and achieves up to 1.62x end-to-end decoding speedup.

title: Rethinking LLM Deployment for Intent-Based Serving
summary: MaverIQ is an intent-based LLM inference serving system that maps user intents to deployment configurations using lightweight fingerprints and analytical models to extrapolate latency and memory. The abstract reports that the system reduces profiling cost by 7-15x and operational cost by 3.8-8.3x compared to baselines.

title: The Economics of AI Decoding Chips: Rebalancing Compute, Capacity, and Bandwidth for Efficient LLM Inference
summary: The paper proposes a rebalanced LLM decoding hardware accelerator (Skymizer HTX-301) using commodity DDR5 memory instead of high-bandwidth memory to lower costs. It demonstrates end-to-end inference serving of DeepSeek-R1 671B at a deterministic 20.3 tokens per second for a fraction of the cost of standard GPU nodes.

title: Efficient and Privacy Aware Edge Cloud Collaborative Inference for Large Language Models
summary: The paper proposes a privacy-centric edge-cloud collaborative LLM inference framework where local endpoints perform speculative decoding and the cloud conducts token verification. It reports reducing per-token latency by up to 46.1% and downlink payloads by up to 67.4% over baseline split inference.

title: GigaWorld-Policy-0.5: A Faster and Stronger WAM Empowered by AutoResearch
summary: The method introduces a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture that separates visual dynamics modeling from action generation to reduce active computation during action-only inference. The abstract reports that this approach preserves training benefits while achieving an 85 ms inference latency on a local RTX 4090 setup.

title: FlexiTensor: Adaptive Multi-Task Deployment of LLMs on Resource-Constrained Heterogeneous Edge Devices
summary: FlexiTensor is an offline planning and deployment system for LLM inference on heterogeneous edge devices that uses non-uniform tensor parallelism, quantization, and evolutionary scheduling to optimize latency under energy budgets. Evaluated on a physical testbed, it accelerates single-task inference by up to 50% and improves multi-task workflow speed by an average of 50%.

title: Smarter and Cheaper at Once: Byte-Exact KV-Cache Grafting Turns a Frozen Small Model into a Verified-Knowledge Flywheel
summary: The method grafts byte-exact key-value (KV) state artifacts into fresh inference contexts, achieving byte-for-byte identical logits to fresh computation. It reduces decode tokens by a factor of 6,574 and expands usable context to 2.8 million tokens at zero extra accelerator memory.

title: Seeing the End at Step Zero: Accelerating Diffusion MLLMs via MLP Sparsity-Aware Truncation
summary: Seer is a training-free framework that accelerates Diffusion MLLMs by detecting the semantic boundary at the first denoising step via MLP activation sparsity and truncating redundant suffixes. It improves batched serving throughput by up to 31x while maintaining or improving accuracy across 9 benchmarks.

title: D-cut: Adaptive Verification Depth Pruning for Batched Speculative Decoding
summary: D-Cut is an adaptive cross-request pruning method for batched speculative decoding that selects draft tokens based on confidence and a runtime cost model. Under high concurrency, it improves average speedup from 1.26x to 1.65x and achieves up to 3.0x speedup over autoregressive decoding on MoE models.

title: Scaling Attention Beyond GPUs for LLM Inference
summary: Beyond is a drop-in runtime that uses a smart offloading scheme and hybrid CPU-GPU attention to process oversized KV caches cooperatively during bandwidth-constrained decoding. It improves scalability, supports longer sequences and larger batch sizes, and outperforms existing sparse attention baselines in efficiency and accuracy.

title: Omnia: Efficient RAG Serving through Speculative Scheduling
summary: Omnia is a RAG serving system that uses chunk speculation with a two-level coarse-to-fine reranking architecture and backpressure-aware scheduling to overlap prefilling and decoding. It maintains near-lossless accuracy while increasing maximum request rate by 57% and reducing average end-to-end latency by 2.99x.

title: VarRate: Training-Free Variable-Rate KV Cache Compression for Long-Context LLMs
summary: VarRate is a training-free KV cache codec that assigns each token a variable low-rank budget based on query salience instead of evicting tokens. It stays within 0.8 points of the uncompressed model on LongBench at a 20% memory budget while significantly reducing prefill overhead.

title: RecGPT-V3 Technical Report
summary: The method introduces a Memory Hub for structured user memory and Latent Intent Reasoning to internalize verbose rationales into compact latent tokens. In large-scale online A/B tests, it cuts end-to-end serving resource consumption by 52.4% and output token cost by 200x while improving commercial metrics.

title: Every Microsecond Matters: Achieving Near Speed-of-Light Latency in GPU Collectives
summary: The method develops low-latency GPU collective communication kernels using barrier-free synchronization and multicast to approach the hardware speed-of-light lower bound. When integrated into LLM inference, these kernels improve inter-token latency and throughput while reducing collective overhead to within 7% of the absolute lower bound.

title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning
summary: Agon introduces a two-stage cascade where one model drafts a solution and another reads it to provide the final answer, using this setup for competitive cross-model RL. At inference, it doubles GRPO's pass@1 on the hard split of DeepMath with Qwen3 compared to GRPO alone.

title: FlashRT: Agent Harness for Guiding Agents to Deploy Real-Time Multimodal Applications
summary: FlashRT is an agent harness that guides coding agents to optimize multi-GPU deployments of multimodal applications. The abstract claims it achieves up to ~70x latency reduction and up to 3.6x throughput improvement across NVIDIA and AMD hardware, reducing latency by 65% for Qwen3-Omni compared to vLLM.

title: LMEdge: QoS-Aware LLM Inference Orchestration on Edge Clusters
summary: LMEdge is a QoS-aware orchestration service that uses machine learning predictors and a lightweight heuristic to dynamically select model configuration and execution placement for edge devices. Evaluated on a Kubernetes testbed with 57 instances, the method reduces latency, preserves accuracy, and improves resource utilization compared to baselines.

title: Transition-Aware Backend Dispatch for Edge LLM Inference
summary: The paper proposes a transition-aware backend dispatch approach that dynamically selects operators for edge LLM inference based on shape and backend-transition costs. Evaluated via trace replay, it reduces latency, energy, and energy-delay product by 17.4%, 14.4%, and 28.5% on average compared to the best static policy.

title: D-NOVA: In-Storage Retrieval Accelerator via Dual-Bound 3D NAND-Optimized Similarity Search with Vector Adaptation
summary: D-NOVA is a hardware-software co-designed in-storage accelerator that executes IVF-based retrieval pipelines directly within 3D NAND memory. It achieves up to 41.7x speedup and 71x energy efficiency over a CPU baseline, and 12.13x higher throughput than state-of-the-art in-storage RAG accelerators.

title: C$^2$KV: Compressed and Composable KV Cache Reuse for Efficient LLM Inference
summary: C2KV introduces a unified framework for non-prefix KV cache reuse that jointly optimizes extraction and concatenation using a lightweight sidecar extractor. It reduces KV cache storage and transfer costs, achieving up to a 17x inference speedup under long contexts while preserving generation quality.

title: Mage-Flow: An Efficient Native-Resolution Foundation Model for Image Generation and Editing
summary: The paper presents Mage-Flow, a compact 4B-scale visual generation stack using a lightweight VAE and CUDA kernel fusion to improve training and inference efficiency. It claims a 2.5x improvement in training throughput and reports that its Turbo variant achieves low-latency inference, generating a 1024x1024 image in 0.59 seconds on an A100 GPU.

title: Generative World Renderer at the Speed of Play
summary: The paper introduces AlayaRenderer-Flash, a real-time generative world renderer reformulated as a few-step autoregressive streaming model with lightweight distilled codecs. The method accelerates rendering from 0.56 FPS to 31.54 FPS, enabling a fully playable 30 FPS generative world.

title: InstantInfer: Enabling Fast LLM Cold Start with Communicating Finite Automata
summary: InstantInfer uses Communicating Finite Automata to safely refactor LLM inference components for concurrent execution and I/O merging, applying this to vLLM cold starts. The abstract reports up to 7.2x speedup for LLM cold-start acceleration across diverse GPUs, workloads, and scales.

title: ABot-World-0: Infinite Interactive World Rollout on a Single Desktop GPU
summary: The paper presents a streaming inference stack with efficient attention, memory-aware scheduling, and low-bit inference for a video world model. The system streams 720P video at up to 16 FPS on a single desktop GPU with 1.2s action-to-first-frame latency and approximately 19GiB peak VRAM.

title: AdaFlash: Adaptive Speculative Decoding via On-Policy Distilled Diffusion Drafters
summary: AdaFlash improves speculative decoding by using on-policy distillation to reduce variance in diffusion drafters and an adaptive length head to dynamically adjust candidate sequence length. The abstract reports that the method achieves up to approximately 66% higher throughput than previous state-of-the-art methods, with significant gains in high-concurrency scenarios.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Speculative Decoding for Diffusion Language Models in Long Document Generation
summary: The paper proposes UASD-DLM, an uncertainty-aware inference scheduler that dynamically adjusts draft depth and context refresh policy for speculative decoding in diffusion language models. It demonstrates improved speed-memory trade-offs and recovery of technical-term accuracy over fixed-depth speculative decoding in a controlled CPU proxy benchmark.
